description_long: This object is a standard urban traffic light assembly featuring three vertically aligned signal lights per unit, with four identical units shown side by side. Each unit has a robust, rectangular black housing with rounded edges and protruding cylindrical visors over each light to shield them from sunlight and improve visibility. The signal lenses are colored red, yellow, and green, with the lights appearing slightly illuminated in some units. The housing is constructed from a durable, weather-resistant material, likely metal or heavy-duty plastic, and is designed to be mounted on a pole or similar structure. The rear and side views reveal a solid, enclosed back with mounting brackets and no visible wiring or control boxes. This type of traffic light is commonly found at intersections to regulate vehicle flow and ensure road safety.
description: A set of four black, vertically stacked traffic lights with red, yellow, and green signal lenses, each housed in a sturdy, weatherproof casing. The units feature cylindrical visors over each light and are designed for pole mounting in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing four identical traffic light units with visible red, yellow, and green lenses and protective visors.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the depth of the housing and the protruding cylindrical visors, with no signal lenses visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the solid, flat backs of the four units, with mounting brackets but no visible lights or control boxes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, highlighting the cylindrical visors and the thickness of the housing.
